column1: #SCHEMA_VERSION=5.6
# SCHEMA v5.2 (2026-04-16) — Added 3 trailing columns: Style
#   Style  : NOM | BOLD | ITALIC | BOLDITALIC               (default per tier: T1=NOM
#   Color  : BLACK | BLUE | GREEN | RED | ORANGE | PURPLE | GREY | WHITE  (default: BLACK)
#   Size   : 2 | 2.5 | 3 | 3.5                              (default per tier: T1=2.5
# Prescribed styles for TAG7 sub-sections (A-F) per ISO 19650 presentation convention:
#   ASS_TAG_7A_TXT  Identity Header              BOLD    BLUE     (2.5)
#   ASS_TAG_7B_TXT  System & Function            ITALIC  GREEN    (2.0)
#   ASS_TAG_7C_TXT  Spatial Context              NOM     ORANGE   (2.0)
#   ASS_TAG_7D_TXT  Lifecycle & Status           NOM     RED      (2.0)
#   ASS_TAG_7E_TXT  Technical Specs              BOLD    PURPLE   (2.0)
#   ASS_TAG_7F_TXT  Classification               ITALIC  GREY     (2.0)
# Tag Style Engine matrix: TAG_{SIZE}{STYLE}_{COLOR}_BOOL  (128 combos = 4 sizes x 4 styles x 8 colors).
# ApplyTagStyleCommand sets exactly one BOOL true per element type; label rows gated by that BOOL become
# visible. Blank Style/Color/Size fields in this CSV mean "inherit tier default" — values are picked up
# by TagFamilyCreatorCommand when generating the label row and by ApplyTagStyleCommand when switching.
# SCHEMA v5.3 (2026-04-22) — Paragraph tiers 1 to 10 (was 1 to 3)
#   Every STING tag family now carries TAG_PARA_STATE_1_BOOL .. TAG_PARA_STATE_10_BOOL as
#   type parameters. TagFamilyCreatorCommand injects all ten via VisibilityParams.
#   SetParagraphDepthCommand supports cumulative depths (1=T1 only
#   Each tier beyond T3 uses the same Calculated Value pattern:
#     Tier row formula template (any Tier N row):
#       if(TAG_PARA_STATE_N_BOOL
#   Recommended use of tiers 4 to 10:
#     T4 — Commissioning & handover   (COMM_* params
#     T5 — Cost & procurement         (CST_*
#     T6 — Carbon & sustainability    (CBN_A1_A3 .. CBN_C3_C4
#     T7 — Fabrication & QC           (ASS_SPOOL_*
#     T8 — Clash & coordination       (CLASH_TRIAGE_*
#     T9 — As-built & health          (ASBUILT_*
#     T10 — Compliance / audit trail  (RGL_*
# Tier column accepts: T1 | T2 | T3 | T4 | T5 | T6 | T7 | T8 | T9 | T10
# Full tag style parameter set injected into every family (TagFamilyCreatorCommand.StyleParams):
#   128 text style BOOLs           TAG_{SIZE}{STYLE}_{COLOR}_BOOL
#                                  SIZES: 2 / 2.5 / 3 / 3.5   STYLES: NOM / BOLD / ITALIC / BOLDITALIC
#                                  COLORS: BLACK BLUE GREEN RED ORANGE PURPLE GREY WHITE
#   Box colour (RGB 0-255)         TAG_BOX_COLOR_R_INT / _G_INT / _B_INT
#   Box visibility / style         TAG_BOX_VISIBLE_BOOL / TAG_BOX_STYLE_TXT  (SOLID | DASHED | NONE | ROUND)
#   Leader colour (RGB 0-255)      TAG_LEADER_COLOR_R_INT / _G_INT / _B_INT
#   Scale / depth cache            TAG_SCALE_TIER_AUTO_BOOL / TAG_DEPTH_TIER_INT
#   Warnings                       TAG_WARN_VISIBLE_BOOL / TAG_WARN_SEVERITY_FILTER_TXT
TAG STYLE PARAMETER CATALOG (v5.3) — Type parameters injected into every family
# Source: StingTools/Core/ParamRegistry.cs — injected by TagFamilyCreatorCommand.StyleParams
# Copy-paste this list when authoring a STING seed tag family by hand. Every
# parameter below is bound as a TYPE parameter on the tag family. ApplyTagStyleCommand
# (visibility BOOLs) and SetBoxColor / SetLeaderColor (RGB ints) flip them at runtime.
# Group breakdown (150 parameters per family):
#   128  TAG_{size}{style}_{color}_BOOL   — text style switch matrix (4 × 4 × 8)
#    10  TAG_PARA_STATE_N_BOOL            — paragraph-depth gates (N=1..10)
#     1  TAG_WARN_VISIBLE_BOOL            — threshold warning toggle
#     1  TAG_WARN_SEVERITY_FILTER_TXT     — warning severity filter
#     3  TAG_BOX_COLOR_{R/G/B}_INT        — tag box colour RGB
#     1  TAG_BOX_VISIBLE_BOOL             — tag box visibility
#     1  TAG_BOX_STYLE_TXT                — tag box style (SOLID | DASHED | NONE | ROUND)
#     3  TAG_LEADER_COLOR_{R/G/B}_INT     — leader colour RGB
#     1  TAG_SCALE_TIER_AUTO_BOOL         — auto-scale tier cache
#     1  TAG_DEPTH_TIER_INT               — current depth tier (cache)

column1: 6J — Tie-In Points (Battery Limits / Spool Terminations / P&ID Break Points)
# TIE-IN POINT TAGS — RESEARCH BASIS & DESIGN RATIONALE
# A "tie-in point" (TIP) is an industry-standard annotation on MEP drawings that
# marks the exact physical location where one system/contract/spool/phase ends and
# another begins.  They appear on:
#   PIPING (Plumbing/Fire/HVAC):
#     • P&ID battery limits — the contractual handover boundary between packages
#     • Phased construction — end of Phase 1 backbone stub
#     • Spool drawings — every spool has two TIP ends (TIP-IN and TIP-OUT)
#     • Isolation valves — the tie-in is the upstream and downstream face
#     ISO/IEC 81346
column2: PFD/P&ID standard: open-ended line symbol with TI reference

column1: #     ISO 10628-2 — P&ID representation of tie-in points as battery limit flags
#   HVAC DUCTS:
#     • Phase 1/2 split on air handling systems (duct stub-out with damper)
#     • Contractor scope split (mechanical contractor vs specialist)
#     SMACNA / CIBSE Guide B — duct termination annotation practice
#   ELECTRICAL (Conduit / Cable Tray):
#     • Conduit stub-up points into future panel boards or equipment bays
#     • Cable tray dead-end requiring future extension
#     IEC 60617 — electrical drawing symbols for future/proposed extensions
#   MULTI-DISCIPLINE (General):
#     • BIM interface points per ISO 19650-2 information container boundaries
#     • Interface register tie-in markers (IFC/Interface Management)
# IN REVIT — why a tag
#   Tie-in points are attributes of existing Revit MEP ELEMENTS (the pipe
#   conduit end).  They do NOT need a separate command to generate them.
#   The correct approach is:
#     1. A dedicated set of tag families — one per discipline/system — that read
#        tie-in parameters from the host element and display them.
#     2. Parameters added to the existing element (not a new element type).
#     3. Tags placed on the EXISTING pipe/duct/conduit at the open end using
#        the standard Revit tag workflow (Tag Studio → Smart Place or manual).
#   This is exactly how the rest of STING tags work — parameters on elements
#   displayed via tag families.  No new command is needed.
# NEW PARAMETERS REQUIRED (to add to PARAMETER_REGISTRY.json):
#   ASS_TIEIN_REF_TXT         — Reference code  e.g. "TI-M-HWS-001"
#   ASS_TIEIN_STATUS_TXT      — OPEN / CONNECTED / CAPPED / DEFERRED
#   ASS_TIEIN_PHASE_TXT       — Construction phase: PH1 / PH2 / PH3
#   ASS_TIEIN_BY_TXT          — Responsible party: MAIN / SUB / FUTURE
#   ASS_TIEIN_SIZE_TXT        — Size at point: DN50 / 400×250 / 32mm conduit
#   ASS_TIEIN_ELEV_TXT        — Elevation AFFL or AFF: "3450 AFFL"
#   ASS_TIEIN_FLOW_DIR_TXT    — SUP / RET / BIDIR / UNKWN (piping/duct direction)
#   ASS_TIEIN_IFC_REF_TXT     — Interface Control Document reference
#   ASS_TIEIN_CONNECTED_BOOL  — 0 = open live tie-in
#   ASS_TIEIN_TAG_1_TXT       — Assembled display tag (auto-populated)
